subject: Biology
title: B Cells and Antibodies
description: A quick, practical guide to B Cells and Antibodies.

hook.text: A single B cell, once activated, can pump out 2,000 antibodies per second.

buildup.text: B cells produce antibodies—Y-shaped proteins that lock onto specific antigens like a key in a lock.

discovery.text: Antibodies neutralize pathogens by blocking them, tagging them for destruction, or clumping them together.

twist.text: Your body can produce over 10 billion different antibody shapes—enough to match almost any pathogen.

climax.text: This diversity comes from gene shuffling during B cell development—random combinations create variety.

punchline.text: 2,000 targeted missiles per second. From one cell.

quiz.question: How many antibodies can an activated B cell produce per second?
